events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes venting trapped pressure to face level (HAZ - hazardous) during manifold operations in an instrumentation & controls scope. This is a serious safety hazard - venting trapped pressure to face level means pressure is being released at worker face/breathing zone level, which is dangerous.

This is a sequence/safety violation. Venting trapped pressure should be done safely - either isolated, bled down through proper vent paths, or with proper PPE/controls before working on the manifold. Venting to face level is a hazard.

Decision: fail - this is dangerous/non-compliant
Risk: high or critical - venting trapped pressure to face level can cause injury (eye, respiratory, pressure blast)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: possibly installed-defective if the vent/bleed path is defective
Percent complete: estimate maybe 40-50%
Findings: trapped pressure vented to face level, hazard
Actions: stop work, isolate/depressurize through proper bleed path, use proper PPE, verify zero energy state
Rationale: venting trapped pressure to face level is a hazard; must depressurize through engineered vent path before continuing.
References: mfr procedures
